text:
Once upon a time in a small forest kingdom, there lived a clever rabbit named Ruby. She loved exploring and gathering berries, but one day she heard ominous songs coming from an ancient oak tree.

Every evening, a ghostly figure appeared—the Phantom Blossom. It wanted Ruby's wisdom. She laughed nervously.

"One day you shall tell us how you found true friendship."

Ruby realized she had a friend. A woodpecker teased her about her tiny nose. "It's the power of a big heart!" Ruby proudly declared.

Years passed. Ruby became queen of the forest. They built a grand castle. The townsfolk were proud; they named the castle Ruby's Rose.

One moonlit night, the Phantom Blossom came again. Ruby saw its glowing head.

"Your heart has wings," the ghost confessed. "Fly far!"

With that secret, Ruby's reign was kind and pure. The forest thrived.

When she tired of flying, Ruby sat on a bench near her castle
